title: centaurea adulterina subsp. numantina
intro: Centaurea adulterina subsp. numantina, also known as mountain cornflower or Spanish cornflower, is a plant species in the daisy family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antipyretic properties. The health benefits of Centaurea adulterina subsp. numantina include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and soothe digestive issues. Centaurea adulterina subsp. numantina is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it can be found in some regions of the Iberian Peninsula.
